channel.title: Definitions on Home Espresso Lab
channel.description: Recent content in Definitions on Home Espresso Lab

item.title: What Is an Affogato? The Italian Dessert That Drowns Gelato in Espresso
item.description: An affogato is a 2-ingredient Italian dessert: a hot espresso shot poured over a scoop of cold vanilla gelato. The word literally means 'drowned' in Italian. Learn what an affogato is, where it comes from, how it differs from a coffee float and milkshake, plus variations, caffeine, calories, and how to order one anywhere.

item.title: What Is Cold Brew? The Complete Definitional Guide to Coffee's Smoothest Drink
item.description: Cold brew is coffee made by steeping coarse-ground beans in cold water for 12–24 hours — never heated. Learn what cold brew actually is, where it comes from (17th-century Japan and the Dutch coffee trade), how it differs from iced coffee, nitro, and French press, plus caffeine, calories, variations, and how to order one anywhere.

item.title: What Is a Cappuccino? The Complete Definitional Guide to Coffee's Most Iconic Italian Drink
item.description: A cappuccino is one part espresso, one part steamed milk, one part foam — but the etymology, the Capuchin-monk origin of the name, and the wet vs dry distinction matter. Learn what's actually in a cappuccino, where the name comes from, how it differs from a latte, flat white, and macchiato, plus caffeine, calories, and how to order one anywhere.

item.title: What Is a Flat White? The Complete Definitional Guide to Coffee's Antipodean Espresso Drink
item.description: A flat white is a small, espresso-forward milk drink with velvety microfoam — born in Australia or New Zealand in the 1980s, depending on whom you ask. Learn what's actually in a flat white, where the name comes from, the disputed origin, how it differs from a latte, cappuccino, cortado, and macchiato, plus caffeine, calories, and how to order one anywhere.

item.title: What Is an Americano? The Complete Definitional Guide to Coffee's Most Misunderstood Drink
item.description: An Americano is espresso diluted with hot water — but the order, the ratio, and the history matter. Learn what's in an Americano, where the name comes from, how it differs from a long black, lungo, and drip coffee, plus caffeine content and how to order one anywhere.
